issue.title: Render GitHub labels as colored chips below the issue title in dep graph nodes

successCriteria.description: FetchedIssue surfaces labels (name + color) — the Rust side already returns them, just type-them in TS
IssueNode renders a row of colored chips matching each label's GitHub color
Hovering a chip shows the label name via the native title attribute
If an issue has >4 labels, only the first 4 chips render and a +N indicator shows the remainder
Issues with no labels render no chip row and no empty space — the node looks unchanged
Variable node heights from #152 still work — chips fit within the node box at all sizes

phases.title: Surface labels via FetchedIssue + render chip row
verification: Read post-edit IssueNode.tsx: chip row renders only when data.labels.length > 0, slice(0,4) bound, overflow indicator with combined name in title
Read post-edit IssueNode.css: chip dimensions 10x6px, border-radius 2px, flex layout with gap
Read post-edit useDependencyGraph.ts: FetchedIssue has labels?, IssueNodeData receives labels: issue.labels ?? [], fallback case has labels: []
Run cargo check — must be clean (no Rust changes expected)
Open Dependency Graph in desktop app: confirm v0.5.2 issues (which have labels: enhancement, desktop, styling, ux on #151) show colored chip rows
Confirm an issue with >4 labels (if any exists in the project history) shows 4 chips + '+N' indicator
Hover a chip in the desktop app: native title tooltip shows the label name
Confirm any issue with no labels shows no chip row and no empty space (regression check)
Confirm variable heights from #152 still work — chips visible at 50/60/75/90px node heights
